comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 7 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.usmobile.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.usmobile.com%2F) ### Comment > It the same screenshot because only 1 is needed. Can't determine what rule/list is doing it but as seen in screenshot usmobile.com is being modified/blocked and should not be. i have unblocked. But felt this needed to be corrected on your end as this is legitimate site i and others need to be able to reach. ### Screenshots

issue.title: www.pinterest.com
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://www.pinterest.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.pinterest.com%2F) ### Comment > So uhh, I got this ad blocker solely for the purpose for trying to remove all, and I mean ALL ads on pinterest as I just have a genuine ick with them, and cannot focus on my work of gathering images and other resources for projects with them. This ad blocker I believe has helped a lot in erasing those ads, however I'm just confused on why some are still able to get by and appear on my feed and whenever I search things up, as on Google, reddit, and other places, this ad blocker is advertised and said by many users to have "solved the entire problem and removed every single ad on pinterest". ### Screenshots

Screenshot 6 ![Screenshot 6](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/iaynqvcg4nk800cc4c0kw4sg4sg4cw05k3kao039bs.jpg?nc=1)

issue.title: Upd #235594 www.cineby.at

comment.body: I would think this rule by itself should be fine `/\/scripts\/[a-z][a-z]\.js$/$script,domain=cineby.at|cineby.*|fmovies.gd|fmovies.*|cineplay.to|cineplay.*`

issue.title: Upd #235594 www.cineby.at

comment.body: @ntnguyen1234 do you think it’s worth keeping `#%#//scriptlet('prevent-addEventListener', '', 'unloaded_at')`? The other rules already block the JS it’s part of, and it's only use I can tell would be as a backup rule.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
Content from Claude Code:

**Question:** Both `safari-totalwar.fandom.com.har` and `chrome-totalwar.fandom.com.har` capture AdGuard blocking ads on `totalwar.fandom.com`. On Safari the anti-adblock wall ("AdShield") fires; on Chrome it doesn't. What's different?

## The site itself

`totalwar.fandom.com` embeds a third-party anti-adblock vendor called **AdShield** (confirmed by string search inside `report.error-report.com`'s own JS bundle). It's loaded via:

```html
<script id="gtrmG" src="https://html-load.com/vendor.js" onload="...">
```

`vendor.js`'s `onload` handler runs a detector, and if it decides ads are blocked, it POSTs to `error-report.com/report?type=loader_light` and injects a full-page wall via `report.error-report.com/modal?...&error=...&domain=html-load.com` — *"Failed to load website properly since html-load.com is blocked. Please allow html-load.com"*. That's the "AdShield triggered" state.

## What's identical in both HARs

Both AdGuard installs block `html-load.com/vendor.js` and the real ad libs (`securepubads.g.doubleclick.net/gpt.js`, `prebid`, `ad-engine`, `gumgum`, `scorecardresearch`, `quantserve`). Same intent, same filter list, on both platforms.

## Where they diverge — the actual smoking gun

| Resource | Safari | Chrome |
|---|---|---|
| `html-load.com/vendor.js` | No network entry at all (blocked pre-request) | 2 entries, `status: 0` (request attempted, then killed) |
| `gpt.js` / `apstag.js` / `gtm.js` | Never requested | `307 Internal Redirect` → `chrome-extension://.../redirects/*.js` |
| `report.error-report.com` (AdShield's own detector bundle, 1.5MB) | Loads, HTTP 200 | Never requested at all |
| `report.error-report.com/modal` + `error-report.com/report` (the wall) | Fires twice | Never fires |

## Why

Chrome's AdGuard blocks via `declarativeNetRequest`, which for well-known ad libs doesn't just drop the request — it **redirects it to a bundled local stub script** (`chrome-extension://e5e81e13.../web-accessible-resources/redirects/googletagservices-gpt.js`, `amazon-apstag.js`, `google-analytics.js`). These are the same benign no-op stand-ins uBlock Origin ships. Result: `window.googletag`, `apstag`, GTM's `dataLayer` all exist and "load successfully" from the page's point of view — nothing errors, nothing looks obviously blocked.

Safari's AdGuard uses Apple's native `WKContentRuleList` content-blocker API, which can only **block** a request outright — it has no mechanism to substitute a fake response. So none of those globals ever get defined, and the block happens early enough that it doesn't even show up as a HAR entry.

That difference is enough that AdShield's bootstrap logic on Safari escalates and loads its full detection/wall bundle from `report.error-report.com` — which never even gets requested on Chrome, so the wall can't fire.

## Bottom line

Chrome's AdGuard fools AdShield with fake stub scripts for the top-tier ad libs (gpt.js/apstag/gtm); Safari's content-blocker architecture can only block, not spoof, so AdShield notices real absence and throws up the wall.

issue.title: totalwar.fandom.com
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://totalwar.fandom.com/wiki/Total_War_Wiki](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftotalwar.fandom.com%2Fwiki%2FTotal_War_Wiki) ### Comment > AdShield still showing up when using Safari + Adguard Mini. However it works with Google Chrome with Adguard extension > > From what Claude mention after analysing the 2 HAR files (safari and chrome) > > ``` > The site itself > > totalwar.fandom.com embeds a third-party anti-adblock vendor called AdShield (confirmed by string search inside report.error-report.com's own JS bundle). It's loaded via: > > <script id="gtrmG" src="https://html-load.com/vendor.js" onload="..."> > > vendor.js's onload handler runs a detector, and if it decides ads are blocked, it POSTs to error-report.com/report?type=loader_light and injects a full-page wall via report.error-report.com/modal?...&error=...&domain=html-load.com ("Failed to load website properly since html-load.com is blocked. Please allow html-load.com"). That's the "AdShield triggered" state. > > What's identical in both HARs > > Both AdGuard installs block html-load.com/vendor.js and the real ad libs (securepubads.g.doubleclick.net/gpt.js, prebid, ad-engine, gumgum, scorecardresearch, quantserve). Same intent, same filter list, on both platforms. > > Where they diverge — the actual smoking gun > > ┌───────────────────────────────┬──────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┐ > │ │ Safari │ Chrome │ > ├───────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤ > │ │ No network │ │ > │ html-load.com/vendor.js │ entry at all │ 2 entries, status: 0 (request │ > │ │ (blocked │ attempted, then killed) │ > │ │ pre-request) │ │ > ├───────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤ > │ gpt.js / apstag.js / gtm.js │ Never │ 307 Internal Redirect → │ > │ │ requested │ chrome-extension://.../redirects/*.js │ > ├───────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤ > │ report.error-report.com │ Loads, HTTP │ │ > │ (AdShield's own detector │ 200 │ Never requested at all │ > │ bundle, 1.5MB) │ │ │ > ├───────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤ > │ report.error-report.com/modal │ │ │ > │ + error-report.com/report │ Fires twice │ Never fires │ > │ (the wall) │ │ │ > └───────────────────────────────┴──────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────┘ > > Why: Chrome's AdGuard blocks via declarativeNetRequest, which for well-known ad libs doesn't just drop the request — it redirects it to a bundled local stub script (chrome-extension://e5e81e13.../web-accessible-resources/redirects/googletagservices-gpt.js, amazon-apstag.js, google-analytics.js). These are the same benign no-op stand-ins uBlock Origin ships. Result: window.googletag, apstag, GTM's dataLayer all exist and "load successfully" from the page's point of view — nothing errors, nothing looks obviously blocked. > > Safari's AdGuard uses Apple's native WKContentRuleList content-blocker API, which can only block a request outright — it has no mechanism to substitute a fake response. So none of those globals ever get defined, and the block happens early enough that it doesn't even show up as a HAR entry. > > That difference is enough that AdShield's bootstrap logic on Safari escalates and loads its full detection/wall bundle from report.error-report.com — which never even gets requested on Chrome, so the wall can't fire. > > Bottom line: Chrome's AdGuard fools AdShield with fake stub scripts for the top-tier ad libs (gpt.js/apstag/gtm); Safari's content-blocker architecture can only block, not spoof, so AdShield notices real absence and throws up the wall. > ``` ``` Username: @ronaldsuwandi ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/lxyrjj1037kwss4k4c84w48o0k4o44ogyxbxe3xrog.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Mini for Mac v2.2.1.1053 System version: | `MacOS 26.5.2 (Build 25F84)` Browser: | Safari License type: | paid Advanced rules: | enabled Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

**Annoyances:**
AdGuard Cookie Notices filter,
AdGuard Popups filter,
AdGuard Other Annoyances filter,
Adblock Warning Removal List,
AdGuard Annoyances filter,
AdGuard Mobile App Banners filter

**Security:**
Online Malicious URL Blocklist,
Phishing URL Blocklist,
Scam Blocklist by DurableNapkin

**Other:**
Filter unblocking search ads and self-promotion
